channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Brock Purdy wants drama-free negotiations with 49ers for expected NFL-record raise
item.description: The 49ers have recently had long, drawn-out contract negotiations with stars. Brock Purdy says he doesn't want to join that list.
item.dc:creator: Cam Inman, The Mercury News

item.title: Sheel Seidler, wife of late Padres owner Peter Seidler, sues brother-in-laws for control of the team
item.description: Sheel Seidler, the wife of late San Diego Padres owner Peter Seidler, has sued two of her brother-in-laws in an attempt to prevent a third Seidler brother from taking control of the team rather than her.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden signs RFK Stadium land bill into law, a step toward potential Commanders stadium in Washington
item.description: President Joe Biden has signed into law a bill that transfers the land that includes the old RFK Stadium from the federal government to city control.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Whyno, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jaguars fire coach Doug Pederson, keep GM Trent Baalke after 'best team assembled' wins just 4 games
item.description: JACKSONVILLE, Fla. (AP) — Jacksonville Jaguars coach Doug Pederson and general manager Trent Baalke never developed enough synergy, if any at all. Pederson thought talent was Jacksonville's bigger issue as losses mounted; Baalke believed coaching was the problem.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tennessee, nation's lone unbeaten, strengthens grip on No. 1; WVU, Michigan, Utah St enter AP Top 25
item.description: Tennessee picked up four more first-place votes and remains at No. 1 for the fifth consecutive week in the AP Top 25 college basketball poll.
item.dc:creator: Dave Skretta, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: NFL coaching moves: Mayo, Pederson are out of a job while Giants elect to keep Daboll
item.description: New England’s Jerod Mayo and Jacksonville’s Doug Pederson are two NFL coaches out of a job while New York Giants coach Brian Daboll is among those who look like they will be back next fall.
item.dc:creator: David Brandt, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Big Ten, SEC each have 4 top 10 teams in AP women's poll as UCLA, South Carolina still lead way
item.description: The Big Ten and Southeastern Conference each has seven teams ranked in the AP top 25 women’s basketball poll, including four apiece in the first 10.
item.dc:creator: Doug Feinberg, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: North Central football team wins third Division III national title, finishes season a perfect 15-0
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — The North Central football dynasty has reached rarified air. So has quarterback Luke Lehnen.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Jahmyr Gibbs tallies 4 touchdowns as Lions take down Vikings to win NFC North, secure No. 1 seed in playoffs
item.description: The NFC North goes to the Detroit Lions, as they took down the Minnesota Vikings to win the division title and be named the No. 1 seed in the playoffs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jimmy Butler wants to leave the Heat. Pat Riley has been in this spot many times before
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Pat Riley turns 80 in a couple of months. The Miami Heat president has not revealed any retirement timetable. He still wears impeccable suits, shakes hands with as firm of a grip as anyone, knows all eyes are on him when he walks into just about any room. He's not stopping.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tampa Bay Buccaneers secure playoff win: Fans celebrate victory
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — With the Bucs clinching a playoff spot, fans are fired up as they look ahead to the next steps in their postseason journey. It was a rough start for fans cheering on the Tampa Bay Buccaneers against the New Orleans Saints on the opening drives.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: NFL playoffs: Lions beat Vikings to secure NFC's No. 1 seed, finalize postseason field
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — The final position in the NFL postseason field was settled in the final game of the regular season. The Detroit Lions earned their week off.
item.dc:creator: LARRY LAGE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Cowboys cheerleader drilled in head by kickoff mishap in final game of season
item.description: A Dallas Cowboys cheerleader was drilled by an errant kickoff by Brandon Aubrey during the team's loss to the Washington Commanders in the final game of the 2024 season
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Baker Mayfield leads Bucs to 4th quarter comeback over Saints to win NFC South title
item.description: Baker Mayfield delivered a clutch fourth quarter to help the Tampa Bay Buccaneers beat the New Orleans Saints, which gives them the NFC South title.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox Sports captures Saints' moment of silence in first game after deadly terror attack
item.description: Fox Sports captured the somber moment when the Tampa Bay Buccaneers and New Orleans Saints had a moment of silence for those killed in the terror attack on New Year's Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Joe Burrow and the Cincinnati Bengals keep their playoff hopes alive by edging the Steelers 19-17
item.description: Joe Burrow passed for 277 yards and a touchdown, Cade York kicked four field goals and the Cincinnati Bengals kept their slim playoff hopes alive with a 19-17 win over the floundering Pittsburgh Steelers on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Heat suspend Jimmy Butler for 7 games and will seek to trade him
item.description: Jimmy Butler was Miami's best player for five years, the leader of a team that went to the NBA Finals twice in his tenure. He talked big and, at times, played bigger. He spoke with reverence for the city, for the franchise, for the famed “Heat Culture.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Postponed Sugar Bowl played with enhanced security about 36 hours after terror attack in New Orleans
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Georgia and Notre Dame fans packed a plaza adjacent to the Superdome and enjoyed music under clear skies — under the watch of snipers on rooftops — before filtering into the stadium for Thursday's College Football Playoff quarterfinal at the Sugar Bowl. “It was a lot of fun.
item.dc:creator: BRETT MARTEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Red Rocks begins 50th season with big dreams
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - After finishing third in the NCAA Nationals last season, the Utah gymnastics team has reloaded and is ready to make a run at its first national title in 30 years. The Red Rocks begin its 50th season Friday at home against Utah State loaded with talent.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Allstate Sugar Bowl postponed
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)—The Caesars Superdome was placed under lockdown Wednesday after a pickup truck drove into a New Year’s crowd about a mile away, killing at least 15 people and injuring dozens, city and federal officials said.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Derrick Rose to open pop-up flower shop in the Loop Thursday
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — Former Chicago Bull and NBA MVP Derrick Rose is opening a pop-up flower shop for one day only in the Loop Thursday. According to a post on Rose's Instagram account, "Rose's Flower Shop" will be open from 12-4 p.m. at 350 North State Street.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Sugar Bowl CFP quarterfinal between Georgia and Notre Dame postponed after deadly truck attack
item.description: The College Football Playoff quarterfinal at the Sugar Bowl between Georgia and Notre Dame was postponed a day because of an attack about a mile away from the Superdome early Wednesday, when authorities say a truck driver deliberately plowed into a New Year’s crowd and killed 15 people.
item.dc:creator: BRETT MARTEL, Associated Press, Fox 5
